root_cause: The orders service was started without CACHE_MAX, so leaky-svc ran with an unbounded in-memory cache (1 MiB chunks). Under continuous load from sreft-load it grew until it hit the 256 MiB cgroup memory limit and was repeatedly OOM-killed (exit 137), causing intermittent checkout failures.
actions_taken: Recreated the sreft-orders container with the same resource limits and network settings as before, but added CACHE_MAX=16 (matching the healthy payments service) so the in-process cache is bounded. Verified the process stayed up without OOM kills and memory usage stabilized around ~42MiB / 256MiB.
postmortem: Blameless postmortem: orders crashed in a restart loop under checkout load because its cache was unbounded while the container had a 256 MiB memory limit. Payments already used CACHE_MAX=16 and stayed healthy, which made the misconfiguration obvious. We restored service by recreating orders with CACHE_MAX=16 (least-risk change, no image rebuild). Follow-ups: make CACHE_MAX required or default to a safe bound in the app; add a compose/env parity check so serve services share the same cache settings; alert on container OOMKilled and restart rate; add a smoke test that fails if the service logs “cache UNBOUNDED”.
